task_completion.description: The 'Liberty & Due Process' collection is selected in the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the left pane of the Jurism interface, the collection named 'Liberty & Due Process' should be highlighted or selected.

task_completion.description: The target Supreme Court cases are visible in the main item list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The middle pane shows the items 'Gideon v. Wainwright', 'Miranda v. Arizona', and 'Obergefell v. Hodges'.

task_completion.description: The tag 'due-process' is applied to Gideon v. Wainwright.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tag 'due-process' is visible in the right-hand 'Tags' tab when 'Gideon v. Wainwright' is selected, or a batch tagging action clearly shows this tag being applied to the item.

task_completion.description: The tag 'due-process' is applied to Miranda v. Arizona.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tag 'due-process' is visible in the right-hand 'Tags' tab when 'Miranda v. Arizona' is selected, or a batch tagging action clearly shows this tag being applied to the item.

task_completion.description: The tag 'due-process' is applied to Obergefell v. Hodges.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The tag 'due-process' is visible in the right-hand 'Tags' tab when 'Obergefell v. Hodges' is selected, or a batch tagging action clearly shows this tag being applied to the item.

integrity.description: The agent used the Jurism graphical user interface to apply the tags.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Jurism application windows, menus, or tag selectors, rather than terminal commands (like sqlite3) modifying the database directly.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Jurism application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface shown must be Jurism (a Zotero-based reference manager), not a text editor, web browser, or mock interface.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a Python script or shell script to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of the agent writing and executing a script (e.g., in VS Code or terminal) to inject the tags into the database.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task takes place in Jurism (a legal reference manager). The target collection is 'Liberty & Due Process'. The target tag to apply is 'due-process'. The collection contains three real US Supreme Court cases that must be tagged: 1) Gideon v. Wainwright, 372 U.S. 335 (1963); 2) Miranda v. Arizona, 384 U.S. 436 (1966); 3) Obergefell v. Hodges, 576 U.S. 644 (2015).
